opp.ais_why:
• Ferid explicitly said he was 'curious to see agentic AI end-to-end automation generating savings without human intervention' and asked whether AI negotiation extends beyond catalog items to services.
• SF description confirms 'interested in Merlin' and the focus for upper-management socialization is 'AI and potential use case for ANA and iRisk in SRPM,' aligning customer pull with Zycus's AI suite.
• One 30-minute intro, deck circulating to executives, demo targeted for late May/early June - early stage but the AI signal is customer-driven, justifying Hungry at Medium confidence.

metrics.narrative: Ferid articulated a clear business driver on the 09 Apr 2026 intro call: post-divestiture standalone entity mandates its own procurement tools separate from parent-company solutions, with focus on cost improvement and cost savings in HR and professional services (his category scope). He stated 'my area is really focused on cost improvement and cost savings' and emphasized the need for process improvement and cost cutting (Avoma intro call 09 Apr). No quantified savings target, baseline spend figure, headcount reduction goal, or cycle-time improvement was stated. The pain is confirmed and category-scoped (HR, professional services, non-catalog sourcing flexibility), but the business case remains qualitative. Quantifying the value case is a critical next move to anchor the demo and justify the investment post-re-engagement.

champion.narrative: No champion identified; single contact at risk of going fully dark. Ferid Znaidia (Sr Category Manager Indirect Procurement) is the sole engaged contact. On the 09 Apr 2026 intro call he was engaged, curious about AI/Merlin agentic capabilities, and committed to sharing the Zycus slide deck with upper management and scheduling a demo for late May or early June. That commitment was never executed. Rep reached out three times (22 Apr, 15 May, 02 Jun 2026) with zero buyer reply, indicating Ferid has either (a) lost interest, (b) been overruled or deprioritized internally, (c) is avoiding the conversation, or (d) is simply unresponsive. No evidence of advocacy (no introductions, no internal championing, no pushing back timeline on our behalf, no sharing of internal docs or committee feedback). No access to Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or procurement leadership. No multi-threading; zero contact roles on the opp. Current engagement status: cold/non-responsive for 78 days. A champion must actively advocate, provide access, and drive the internal agenda; Ferid has done none of those since the intro call. The deal is single-threaded to a contact who has gone silent, which is a critical vulnerability and the primary reason the deal is Off Track.

sources: Avoma intro call 09 Apr 2026, Ferid confirmed: 'Yes' to using Ariba
SF Competitors__c: SAP Ariba
Avoma intro call 09 Apr 2026: Ferid's questions about non-catalog AI automation suggest Ariba gap
competition.narrative: Competing against SAP Ariba (the incumbent). Ferid confirmed on the 09 Apr 2026 intro call that Commscope uses SAP S/4HANA and Ariba ('do you use Ariba as well? Yes'). Ariba is the entrenched eProcurement and sourcing platform, so Zycus is the challenger seeking to displace or augment it. Ferid did not state dissatisfaction with Ariba, but his questions about AI-driven automation for non-catalog sourcing and services (HR, professional services, templates) suggest Ariba's catalog-centric, purchase-order-first model is a poor fit for his category scope. No mention of other vendors being evaluated (no Coupa, GEP, Jaggaer, Ivalua, or niche solutions). The competitive positioning is Zycus Merlin agentic AI (end-to-end automation, autonomous negotiation, intake orchestration, non-catalog flexibility, organically built suite) vs Ariba's established footprint and SAP integration. The wedge is AI depth, services/non-catalog fit, and the post-divestiture forcing function (need for standalone tools). However, with 78 days of buyer silence and no demo executed, Zycus has made zero progress against the incumbent, and the status-quo (continue with Ariba, defer the evaluation, or abandon the initiative) is the most likely current threat.

identify_pain.narrative: Operational and financial pain is clearly articulated and category-scoped. Post-divestiture from parent company, Commscope is now a standalone entity within Amphenol and must implement its own procurement tools separate from legacy parent solutions (Ferid: 'we're still a standalone company, we have to have our own tools'). Current state: using SAP S/4HANA and Ariba for catalog procurement, but heavily manual processes for non-catalog indirect sourcing (HR, professional services, templates, consulting); manual supplier scorecards; no intake/orchestration automation. Pain owner is Ferid Znaidia (Sr Category Manager Indirect Procurement), who owns HR and professional services categories and is accountable for cost improvement and cost savings in those areas. The pain is compounded by the divestiture forcing a tool-stack refresh and by the focus on non-catalog, services-heavy sourcing where Ariba's catalog-first model is a poor fit. Ferid expressed strong interest in AI-driven end-to-end automation to reduce manual work and drive savings without human intervention. The pain is real, confirmed, and scoped, but the urgency and prioritization vs other post-divestiture initiatives is unclear given the 78-day silence.

sources: Avoma intro call 09 Apr 2026, Ferid: 'usually stuff like this takes 6 months'
Avoma intro call 09 Apr 2026, Ferid: 'post-divestiture, standalone, part of Amphenol now'
No further contracting/legal/InfoSec discussion
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec requirements, and implementation scoping are entirely unknown. No discussion of MSA/SOW, no mention of legal/compliance approval, no SI/partner involvement stated, and no implementation plan beyond Ferid's general comment that 'stuff like this usually takes up to 6 months'. Whether Commscope runs vendor onboarding through a centralized procurement function, whether InfoSec reviews all SaaS tooling, and whether the deal would flow through a reseller or direct are all gaps. The post-divestiture context (standalone entity, own procurement tools, part of Amphenol now) adds complexity: it is unclear whether Amphenol procurement policies apply or whether Commscope retains autonomy. Paper process visibility requires multi-threading to procurement leadership, IT, and legal, which cannot happen until the buyer re-engages.

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer identified. Ferid Znaidia (Sr Category Manager Indirect Procurement) is the single engaged contact; he manages HR and professional services categories and stated he would share the Zycus slide deck with 'upper management' to gauge interest (Avoma intro call 09 Apr). Who holds budget authority, who approves a $250k investment, and whether the decision sits with procurement leadership (e.g. the VP Procurement level per account contact list) or finance/operations is unknown. Zero contact roles on the opp, no CFO/sponsor/exec participation on calls, and no discussion of budget approval process or commercial authority. The 78-day silence suggests either (a) upper management was not engaged or declined to pursue, or (b) Ferid lacks the authority or urgency to drive the evaluation forward. Mapping the Economic Buyer and understanding the approval chain is the foundational next step after re-engagement.

decision_process.narrative: No decision process mapped. The call sequence shows only the 09 Apr 2026 intro call with Ferid (Sr Category Manager); no follow-on calls, no stakeholder escalation, no committee formation. Ferid stated he would share the Zycus slide deck with upper management to gauge interest and then schedule a demo for late May or early June, but that step never happened (rep attempted three times to schedule, zero buyer reply). Whether upper management reviewed the materials, whether there is a formal procurement evaluation committee, whether IT/InfoSec/finance must approve, and what the approval chain looks like is entirely unknown. The 78-day silence could mean (a) no internal approval to proceed, (b) deprioritized vs other initiatives, (c) Ferid lacks authority to advance alone, or (d) timing/budget constraints. The process is a black box; re-engagement must map it before any demo is scheduled.

decision_criteria.narrative: Ferid articulated clear evaluation priorities on the 09 Apr 2026 intro call: AI-driven end-to-end automation generating savings without human intervention (he asked 'how does it go from A to Z without human intervention and come up with savings at the end'), applicability to non-catalog sourcing for HR and professional services (not just tangible/catalog items), cost of subscription, implementation timeline (noted 'usually stuff like this takes up to 6 months'), and modules/functionality alignment (eProcurement AI orchestration, spend analytics, supplier risk/performance management, automated negotiation agent). He confirmed familiarity with RFX/RFP/RFQ sourcing processes and manual supplier scorecards, signaling those as current-state pain points. No formal RFP, no weighted scorecard, no technical/security/integration criteria stated, and no mention of competitive bake-off structure. The criteria are inferred from discovery questions and stated interests, not from a buyer-issued BRD or RFP document. Whether these reflect the decision committee's full criteria or only Ferid's category-level view is unknown given the single-thread and 78-day silence.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AI Curious tier per intro-call evidence. Ferid expressed strong interest in agentic AI capabilities (A-to-Z automation without human intervention, savings generation, applicability to services sourcing) and asked how the technology actually works in practice. He stated 'I'm curious to see how it goes from A to Z without human intervention and comes up with savings' and 'are you interested in AI or is that something you're ? I don't know, that's a new thing.' The curiosity is genuine and aligns with his cost-improvement mandate, but no AIS score is logged in Salesforce, and the 78-day silence suggests the curiosity did not translate into action. The AI appetite is there but dormant; reactivating it with a concrete use-case demo and ROI anchor is the path forward if the deal re-engages.

items.action: Send executive re-qualification message to Ferid and multi-thread to procurement leadership.
items.trigger: Ferid unresponsive to three outreach attempts over 78 days; last attempt 02 Jun 2026 (~24 days ago); deal cold since 09 Apr intro call.
items.expected_effect: Re-engage with a concise, value-led message acknowledging the silence and offering a single 30-minute AI ROI call focused on HR services sourcing savings (Ferid's category). Simultaneously reach out via LinkedIn or email to at least one procurement leader from the account contact list (e.g. the Director Global Procurement role) to validate priority and multi-thread. If no reply within 10 business days, escalate to the deal owner's manager for executive-to-executive re-qualification or mark the deal dormant to free capacity for live opportunities.

items.action: Build and send a one-page AI ROI model for HR services sourcing.
items.trigger: Ferid raised cost concern and asked for cost estimate on 09 Apr intro call; no quantified business case or ROI provided in 78 days.
items.expected_effect: Create a tailored ROI model showing projected savings from Merlin Intake orchestration automation, Anna autonomous negotiation on HR/professional services tail spend, and manual-process elimination (scorecard automation, RFX cycle time reduction). Anchor to Ferid's stated priorities (cost improvement, savings, non-catalog sourcing) and the post-divestiture forcing function (standalone tools needed). Attach to the re-engagement message as a concrete value anchor to justify the demo and counter status-quo inertia.

items.action: Identify and reach the Economic Buyer or procurement sponsor.
items.trigger: $250k opportunity with no identified budget authority, no EB mapped, and single-threaded to category manager (Ferid) who has gone silent for 78 days.
items.expected_effect: Research the procurement org structure (account contacts include the VP Procurement role and Director Global Procurement); validate via LinkedIn or Zycus network whether either holds budget authority for a $250k procurement-platform investment. Attempt a direct reach (email or LinkedIn) framing the post-divestiture tooling need and AI-driven savings opportunity as a strategic initiative requiring executive visibility. If Ferid re-engages, ask him to facilitate an introduction; if he does not, this outreach may bypass the single-thread and validate whether the opportunity is real or whether it has been shelved internally.

items.action: Confirm whether the evaluation has been deprioritized or shelved internally.
items.trigger: 78-day buyer silence after engaged intro call; Ferid's upper-management share step never confirmed; no feedback on whether the initiative is still active.
items.expected_effect: In the re-engagement message, explicitly ask whether the procurement-tooling evaluation is still a priority or whether it has been deferred due to post-divestiture complexity, budget constraints, or competing initiatives. A direct, low-pressure question ('Has the timing shifted, or should we reconnect later in the year?') gives Ferid or a multi-threaded contact an easy out and surfaces the truth. If deprioritized, mark the deal as dormant with a future re-engagement trigger (e.g. Q1 2027 budget cycle) rather than investing further effort in a dead opportunity.

items.action: If no response in 10 business days, escalate to executive connect for re-qualification.
items.trigger: If ranks 1-4 produce no buyer reply after 10 business days, the deal is confirmed dead and requires executive-level intervention or closure decision.
items.expected_effect: The deal owner's manager conducts an executive-to-executive outreach to a procurement or finance leader at Commscope, positioning the conversation as a strategic check-in on post-divestiture procurement transformation and AI-driven savings opportunities (not a sales push). The goal is to either (a) validate that the opportunity is real and re-open the dialogue at a higher level, or (b) confirm it is shelved and close the opportunity to free capacity. This is a final re-qualification step before marking the deal lost or dormant.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 09 Apr 2026, 78 days in Qualified stage, 219 days to close (31 Jan 2027). Last verified BUYER engagement was the intro call on 09 Apr 2026 (~78 days ago). Rep attempted re-engagement on 22 Apr, 15 May, and 02 Jun 2026 (~24 days ago) with zero buyer reply. No deliverable executed, no two-way conversation, and no buyer-facing milestone met in 78 days. Cold for 60+ days forces Off Track regardless of stage or close proximity.

Rep outreach 15 May 2026: scheduling deep-dive discovery, pulling in additional stakeholders (SF Next_Step__c 15 May).

north_star_verdict.headline: 102 days since intro call, 24 days since last outreach attempt with zero buyer response. Three consecutive rep attempts ignored. No demo, no stakeholder expansion, no two-way engagement. Deal remains cold and unresponsive at EARLY stage.

flags: No Economic Buyer or Decision Maker mapped: $250k investment with no identified budget authority, no CFO/sponsor engagement, and no approval-chain visibility. Ferid's 'upper management' share step was never confirmed. Before any demo, map who controls the commercial decision and validate the business case at that level.
best_practice_check.summary: Single-threaded to unresponsive contact for 78 days, zero multi-threading or power-role access, and no champion behavior after initial curiosity; deal is cold and requires executive-led re-qualification before any further investment.

competitive_position.summary: Competing against SAP Ariba (incumbent, integrated with S/4HANA, entrenched eProcurement and sourcing platform). Ariba is the baseline; Zycus is the challenger. The wedge is Merlin agentic AI for end-to-end automation without human intervention, non-catalog/services sourcing fit (HR, professional services), and the post-divestiture forcing function (standalone tools needed). However, with 78 days of buyer silence, zero demo, and no multi-threading, Zycus has made no progress against the incumbent. The most immediate threat is status-quo inertia: Ferid's silence likely means either internal deprioritization, upper-management disinterest, or Ariba retention by default. Ariba threat level is HIGH not because of active competitive defense but because the buyer has not engaged to validate an alternative, leaving the incumbent in place unchallenged.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: Ferid confirmed 'Yes' when asked if Commscope uses Ariba; intro call 09 Apr 2026
competitors.how_we_win: Anchor the Merlin agentic AI story (A-to-Z automation without human intervention, autonomous negotiation agent Anna, intake orchestration in Teams) to Ferid's stated priority: cost improvement and savings in non-catalog HR/professional services sourcing where Ariba's catalog-first model underperforms. Use the post-divestiture mandate (standalone tools) as the forcing function, quantify the ROI on manual-process elimination (scorecard automation, intake, RFX cycle time), and multi-thread to procurement leadership and the Economic Buyer to break the single-point-of-contact silence and validate urgency.

competitors.name: Status Quo / Do Nothing

competitors.how_we_win: Re-engage with a value-led executive message: acknowledge the post-divestiture complexity, anchor to a single high-impact use case (e.g. AI-driven savings in HR services sourcing), offer a condensed 30-minute ROI-focused call with a Merlin demo snippet, and multi-thread to at least one procurement or finance leader to validate priority. If no response within 10 business days, escalate to the deal owner's manager for an executive-to-executive re-qualification conversation before marking the deal dormant.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Strong buyer curiosity expressed on intro call but zero follow-through; AI differentiation wedge not activated. Ferid was highly engaged with the Merlin agentic AI narrative (end-to-end automation, autonomous negotiation, no human intervention, savings generation) and asked detailed questions about how it works A-to-Z. He specifically inquired about AI applicability to non-catalog services sourcing (his category scope) and expressed interest in seeing the technology in action. However, the demo was never delivered, no ROI model or proof point was provided, and the differentiation story was not reinforced post-intro call. The 78-day silence suggests the AI positioning did not translate into urgency or internal advocacy. If the deal re-engages, lead with a high-impact Merlin demo (Intake in Teams, Anna autonomous negotiation on HR services sourcing) anchored to a quantified savings case to justify the evaluation and displace Ariba inertia.

items.quote: Ferid: 'I'm curious how it goes A to Z without human intervention and comes up with savings at the end' (Avoma intro call 09 Apr 2026)
items.criterion: AI-driven end-to-end automation generating savings without human intervention

items.criterion: Non-catalog sourcing flexibility for HR and professional services

items.quote: Ferid: 'all comes down to the cost; usually stuff like this takes 6 months' (Avoma intro call 09 Apr 2026); no pricing or ROI provided yet
items.criterion: Cost of subscription and implementation timeline

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong product-to-pain alignment on AI automation, non-catalog sourcing, and post-divestiture tooling needs, but zero validation post-intro call. Zycus Merlin agentic platform (intake orchestration, autonomous negotiation, end-to-end automation) directly addresses Ferid's stated interests and current-state gaps (manual processes, no intake module, catalog-only Ariba fit). However, the 78-day silence suggests either misalignment on timing/priority, cost concerns, internal resistance, or Ferid's lack of authority to advance alone.

hard.ais_why:
• Ferid explicitly said he was 'curious to see agentic AI end-to-end automation generating savings without human intervention' and asked whether AI negotiation extends beyond catalog items to services.
• SF description confirms 'interested in Merlin' and the focus for upper-management socialization is 'AI and potential use case for ANA and iRisk in SRPM,' aligning customer pull with Zycus's AI suite.
• One 30-minute intro, deck circulating to executives, demo targeted for late May/early June - early stage but the AI signal is customer-driven, justifying Hungry at Medium confidence.

value.name: SAP Ariba
value.quote: Ferid confirmed 'Yes' when asked if Commscope uses Ariba; intro call 09 Apr 2026
value.how_we_win: Anchor the Merlin agentic AI story (A-to-Z automation without human intervention, autonomous negotiation agent Anna, intake orchestration in Teams) to Ferid's stated priority: cost improvement and savings in non-catalog HR/professional services sourcing where Ariba's catalog-first model underperforms. Use the post-divestiture mandate (standalone tools) as the forcing function, quantify the ROI on manual-process elimination (scorecard automation, intake, RFX cycle time), and multi-thread to procurement leadership and the Economic Buyer to break the single-point-of-contact silence and validate urgency.

value.name: Status Quo / Do Nothing
value.quote: Rep: '6/2/26 MQ Sending out 3rd request for discovery meeting. Ferid has not responded to multiple attempts'; 78 days cold since intro call
value.how_we_win: Re-engage with a value-led executive message: acknowledge the post-divestiture complexity, anchor to a single high-impact use case (e.g. AI-driven savings in HR services sourcing), offer a condensed 30-minute ROI-focused call with a Merlin demo snippet, and multi-thread to at least one procurement or finance leader to validate priority. If no response within 10 business days, escalate to the deal owner's manager for an executive-to-executive re-qualification conversation before marking the deal dormant.
